title: Replication script for the 4Smethod
text: Identification scale’s subdimensions first preliminary step consists structuring measurement scale unidimensional subdimensions (also called latent traits) comply three central assumptions: unidimensionality: items subdimension represent homogeneous latent trait, conditional independence: latent trait capture whole correlation across items remaining residual correlation items, increasing monotonicity: higher item level consistently reflect higher underlying trait level. followed recommended strategy reported PROMIS (Patient-Reported Outcomes Measurement Information System) initiative Reeve et al. (2007) [1] cross-sectional data. adapted PROMIS method longitudinal data using resampling strategy: randomly selected single visit per subject create pseudo independent sample size N PROMIS method used, replicated procedure multiple times account sampling fluctuations. Note three calibration assumptions may successively evaluated stepbacks systematic reevaluations measure impact certain decisions. Illustration replicate 1:
code: R <- 50 # number of replicates  seeds <- sample(x=1:1000,size=R) r <- 1 # replicate  set.seed(seeds[r]) # seed  # random selection of one visit per patient select <- sapply(X=table(data$ID),                   FUN=function(x){sample(1:x,size=1)}) data_r <- data[which(data$ID==1),][select[1],] for(i in 2:N)   data_r <- rbind(data_r,data[which(data$ID==i),][select[i],])

title: Replication script for the 4Smethod
text: explanatory factorial analysis (EFA) performed item data identify independent subdimensions measured scale. optimal number subdimensions can determined dressing scree plot successive eigenvalues chosen largest either largest number factors successive eigenvalues greater value 1 (Kaiser criterion), identifying “elbow” eigenvalues begin plateau (Cattell criterion). analysis can carried using function fa.parallel() R-package psych.  , analysis suggests 2 factors.
code: par(mar=c(4.5,4.5,2,0),cex=.8) screeplot <- fa.parallel(data_r[,c(paste("item",1:K,sep=""))],                          fm = 'minres', fa = 'fa', cor='poly', plot=TRUE) #> Parallel analysis suggests that the number of factors =  2  and the number of components =  NA

title: Replication script for the 4Smethod
text: Items assigned subdimension contribute based factor loadings resulting polychoric correlation matrix, rotation maximizing within-factor variance (ensuring item strongly associated assigned dimension weakly others), representing strength association items latent traits. assume item contributes one subdimension. higher loading item lower 0.3, contribution considered small assigned subdimension. Item assignment: dimension 1 (blue) items 1 4 dimension 2 (red) items 5 10.
code: pc <- hetcor(data_r[,c(paste("item",1:K,sep=""))], ML=TRUE)       efa2 <- fa(r=pc$correlations, nfactors=2, rotate="varimax")  contrib2 <- data.frame(ITEM = paste("item",1:K,sep=""),                        FACT1 = efa2$loadings[,2],                        FACT2 = efa2$loadings[,1]) contrib2$max_loading <- apply(X=contrib2[,-1],MARGIN=1,FUN=max) contrib2$FACTOR <- ifelse(contrib2$max_loading == contrib2$FACT1,1,2) contrib2$FACTOR[which(contrib2$max_loading<.3)] <- NA  par(mar=c(4,4,2,1),cex=.8) barplot(cbind(FACT1,FACT2)~ITEM,         data=contrib2, beside=TRUE, col=c("blue","red"),         space = c(.1,2),ylim=c(-.1,1.2), xlab = "item",         las=1,names.arg=1:K,main="Items' contribution to 2 dimensions") abline(h=0.3,lwd=2,lty=3,col="grey40")  abline(h=0,lwd=2,lty=1,col="black") legend(x="topright",legend=paste("dimension ",1:2,sep=""),col=c("blue","red"),pch=16,bty="n")

title: Replication script for the 4Smethod
text: confirmatory factorial analysis (CFA) performed identified subdimensions evaluate model fit. According Reeve et al. (2007), model fit considered sufficient following criteria satisfied: comparative fit index (CFI) > 0.95, Tucker Lewis index (TLI) > 0.95, root means square error approximation (RMSEA) < 0.06, standardized root mean square residual (SRMR) < 0.08. CFI TLI assess fit estimated model null model assuming constraint structure. RMSEA SRMR assess fit estimated model observed data. analysis can carried using function cfa() R-package lavaan. fit criteria respected.
code: rep2 <- paste(paste(" dim1 =~ ",paste(paste("item",1:5,sep=""),collapse="+"),"\n"),               paste(" dim2 =~ ",paste(paste("item",6:11,sep=""),collapse="+")),sep="")  fit2 <- cfa(rep2,              data = data_r,              ordered = paste("item",1:K,sep="")) fitMeasures(object=fit2,fit.measures=c("cfi","tli","rmsea","srmr")) #>   cfi   tli rmsea  srmr  #> 1.000 1.000 0.000 0.031

text: correlation matrix CFA predicted values observations reported identify residual correlation. PROMIS authors Reeve et al. (2007) [1] consider two items substantially correlated residual correlation exceeds 0.2. case two correlated items assigned subdimension, one removed, considered redundant according clinical relevance. , redundancy observed.

text: monotonicity visually inspected item plotting mean item levels decile rest-score assigned subdimension (.e., sum-score items subdimension except one), item higher-level probabilities decile rest-score. mtext(text = "response", cex = .5, side = 4, line = 2.5)    }  # dimension 2 for(i in 1:6){      plot(y = monot_D2$results[[i]][[2]][,"P(X >=1)"],         x = monot_D2$results[[i]][[2]][,"Hi Score"],        type = "l", col = col[1], ylim = c(0,1), xlim = c(0,(6-1)*(nl-1)), las = 1,        bty = "l", xlab = "rest-score", ylab = "probability")   mtext(text = paste("item",5+i,sep=""), cex = .5, side = 3, line = 0, font = 2, col = "red")   for(m in 2:4)     lines(y = monot_D2$results[[i]][[2]][,paste("P(X >=",m,")",sep="")],            x = monot_D2$results[[i]][[2]][,"Hi Score"], col = col[m])   par(new=TRUE)   plot(y = monot_D2$results[[i]][[2]][,"Mean"], x = monot_D2$results[[i]][[2]][,"Hi Score"],         lwd = 2, type = "l", col = "black", lty = 2, xlim=c(0,(6-1)*(nl-1)), ylim=c(0, nl-1),        xlab="",ylab="",yaxt="n",bty="l",legend=NULL,cex.lab=1.5,cex.axis=1.5,axes = F)     axis(4, line=0, las=1); mtext(text = "response", cex = .5, side = 4, line = 2.5)    }  # legend plot(x = c(0,1), y = c(0,1), col="white",main="", xlab = "", ylab = "",bty = "n", axes = FALSE) legend(x="center",bty="n",cex=1,lwd=1,        legend=c(paste("obs P(Y >=",1:(nl-1),")",sep=""),"obs mean response"),        text.col=c(col,"black"),col=c(col,"black"),lty=c(rep(1,nl-1),2))

title: Replication script for the 4Smethod
text: final structure determined aggregation replicates. Items consistently assigned subdimension assigned . Items repeatedly unassigned subdimension excluded analysis provide additional information. Items straddling two subdimensions reviewed assigned based clinical relevance subdimension loadings.  Final item assignment:
code: REPL <- list(NULL)  for(r in 1:R){ # for each replicate      set.seed(seeds[r]) # seed    # random selection of one visit per patient   select <- sapply(X=table(data$ID),                     FUN=function(x){sample(1:x,size=1)})   data_r <- data[which(data$ID==1),][select[1],]   for(i in 2:N)     data_r <- rbind(data_r,data[which(data$ID==i),][select[i],])    # item contributions   pc <- hetcor(data_r[,c(paste("item",1:K,sep=""))], ML=TRUE)      efa2 <- fa(r=pc$correlations, nfactors=2, rotate="varimax")    contrib2 <- data.frame(ITEM = paste("item",1:K,sep=""),                          FACT1 = efa2$loadings[,2],                          FACT2 = efa2$loadings[,1])   contrib2$max_loading <- apply(X=contrib2[,-1],MARGIN=1,FUN=max)   contrib2$FACTOR <- ifelse(contrib2$max_loading == contrib2$FACT1,1,2)   contrib2$FACTOR[which(contrib2$max_loading<.3)] <- NA    # save   REPL[[r]]$contrib <- contrib2   if(r != R)     REPL <- c(REPL,list(NULL)) } par(mfrow=c(1,2),mar=c(4.5,4,.5,1),cex=.8)  # order : per replicates contrib <- sapply(REPL, function(x) x$contrib$FACTOR) plot(contrib, col = c("blue","red"),       key = NULL, xlab = "replicates", ylab = "item", main ="", las = 1)  # order : per percentage % over replicates contrib_bydim <- t(apply(contrib,1,function(x){table(factor(x,levels=1:2))})) contrib_bydim_matrix <- t(apply(contrib_bydim,1,function(x){rep.int(1:2,times=x)}))  plot(contrib_bydim_matrix, col = c("blue","red"),       key = NULL, polygon.cell = list(border=FALSE), axis.col = NULL,       xlab = "percentage of replicates", ylab = "item", main ="", las = 1) segments(x0=.5, y0=seq(from=.5,to=K+.5,by=1), x1=75.5, y1=seq(from=.5,to=K+.5,by=1)) segments(x0=R+.5, y0=.5, x1=R+.5, y1=K+.5) axis(1,line=0,lwd=1,labels=c("0%","25%","50%","75%","100%"),      at=c(0,R/4,R/2,R*3/4,R)+.5,cex.axis=1) # dimension 1 item_num_d1 <- 1:5 item_lab_d1 <- paste("item",item_num_d1,sep="") ni1 <- length(item_num_d1) dim_col_d1 <- "blue" item_col_d1 <- paletteer::paletteer_c("ggthemes::Green-Blue Diverging",n=ni1)  # dimension 2 item_num_d2 <- 6:11 item_lab_d2 <- paste("item",item_num_d2,sep="") ni2 <- length(item_num_d2) dim_col_d2 <- "red" item_col_d2 <- paletteer::paletteer_c("ggthemes::Sunset-Sunrise Diverging",n=ni2+1)[-1]

title: Replication script for the 4Smethod
text: Description subdimension progression trajectory dimension continuum underlying repeated item data modeled time repeated item data using joint Item Response Theory (IRT) model adapted ordinal repeated measures time--event data [2]. model composed longitudinal submodel survival submodel. parameter simultaneously estimated maximum likelihood using R-package JLPM (available CRAN https://github.com/VivianePhilipps/JLPM). longitudinal submodel combines: - linear mixed structural model describe underlying dimension deterioration time according covariates functions time, fixed effects defining mean dimension trajectory population level individual correlated random effects capturing individual deviations; - item-specific cumulative probit measurement model define link underlying dimension item observation. survival submodel proportional hazard survival model (cause-specific proportional hazard model case competing causes) adjusted current underlying dimension level linear predictor account informative dropout induced event. details, please refer Saulnier et al. [2].

PREDICTED TRAJECTORIES  # dimension 1 par(mar=c(4.5,4,1,1))  plot(x = pred_D1$times$t, y = pred_D1$pred$Ypred[which(pred_D1$pred$Yname==item_lab_d1[1])],       col = item_col_d1[1], lwd = 2, type = "l", xlim = range(time), ylim = c(0,nl-1),       bty = "n", xlab = "Time", ylab = "Item level", main = "Dimension 1", col.main = "blue") for(i in 2:ni1)   lines(x = pred_D1$times$t, y = pred_D1$pred$Ypred[which(pred_D1$pred$Yname==item_lab_d1[i])],          col = item_col_d1[i], lty = 1, lwd = 2) legend(x = "bottomright", lty = 1, lwd = 1, col = item_col_d1[1:ni1], legend = item_lab_d1,         bty = "n", cex = .8, ncol = 2)  # title: WaldMult Performs multivariate Wald tests. — WaldMult
text: 1. H0 : theta_m = (theta_j1, ..., theta_jm)' = 0    ie, tests subset m parameters simultaneously equal zero.    Wald statistic w = theta_m' * Var(theta_m)^(-1) * theta_m    associated p-value obtained p = P(chi2_(dof=m) > w) 2. H0 : theta = c1*theta_j1 + ... + cm*theta_jm = 0    ie, tests combination m parameters equal zero.    Wald statistic w = theta^2 / Var(theta)                            = (c1*theta_j1 + ... + cm*theta_jm)^2 / Var(c1*theta_j1 + ... + cm*theta_jm)    associated p-value obtained p = P(chi2_(dof=1) > w) 3. H0 : Theta = *coef = 0 matrix m lines n columns.    ie, tests multiple combinations parameters simultaneously equal zero.    Wald statistic w = Theta * Var(Theta)^(-1) * Theta'                            = (*coef)' * (*vcov*')^(-1) * (*coef)    associated p-value obtained p = P(chi2_(dof=m) > w) function can applied object coef

what: Estimation of latent process joint models for multivariate longitudinal outcomes and time-to-event data. — jointLPM
title: Estimation of latent process joint models for multivariate longitudinal outcomes and time-to-event data. — jointLPM
text: function fits extended joint models shared random effects. longitudinal submodel handles multiple continuous longitudinal outcomes (Gaussian  non-Gaussian, curvilinear) well ordinal longitudinal outcomes mixed effect framework. model assumes outcomes measure underlying latent process defined common factor, outcome related latent common factor outcome-specific measurement model whose nature depends type outcome (linear model Gaussian outcome, curvilinear model non-Gaussian outcome, probit model binary outcome, cumulative probit model ordinal outcome). latent process level, model assumes linear mixed model. survival submodel handles right-censored (possibly left-truncated) time--event competing causes association longitudinal survival data captured including random effect mixed model predicted current level underlying process linear predictors cause-specific proportional hazard survival model. Parameters measurement models, latent process mixed model survival model simultaneously estimated using maximum likelihood method, Marquardt-Levenberg optimization algorithm.

text: fixed two-sided linear formula object specifying fixed-effects linear mixed model latent process level. response outcomes separated + left ~ covariates separated + right ~. identifiability purposes, intercept specified default removed -1. Variables contrast different outcomes also estimated included contrast(). random one-sided formula random-effects latent process mixed model. Covariates random-effect separated +. intercept always included identifiability. subject name covariate representing grouping structure. idiag optional logical variance-covariance structure random-effects. FALSE, non structured matrix variance-covariance considered (default). TRUE diagonal matrix variance-covariance considered. cor optional indicator inclusion autocorrelated Gaussian process linear mixed model latent process level. Option BM(time) indicates brownian motion parameterized variance option AR(time) specifies autoregressive process continuous time parameterized variance correlation intensity. cases, time variable representing measurement times. default, autocorrelated Gaussian process added. link optional vector families parameterized link functions defining measurement models (one outcome). Option "linear" (default) specifies linear link function. possibilities include "beta" estimating link function family Beta cumulative distribution functions, "Splines" approximating link function -splines "thresholds" ordinal outcomes modelled cumulative probit models. splines case, number nodes nodes location also specified. number nodes first entered followed -, location specified "equi", "quant" "manual" respectively equidistant nodes, nodes quantiles marker distribution interior nodes entered manually argument intnodes. followed -splines. example, "7-equi-splines" means -splines 7 equidistant nodes, "6-quant-splines" means -splines 6 nodes located quantiles marker distribution "9-manual-splines" means -splines 9 nodes, vector 7 interior nodes entered argument intnodes. intnodes optional vector interior nodes. argument required -splines link function nodes entered manually. epsY optional positive real used rescale marker (0,1) beta link function used. default, epsY=0.5. randomY optional logical including outcome-specific random intercept. FALSE outcome-specific random intercept added (default). TRUE independent outcome-specific random intercepts parameterized variance included. var.time name variable representing measurement times. survival two-sided formula object. left side formula corresponds Surv() object right-censored (Surv(EntryTime,Time,Indicator)) possibly left-truncated (Surv(EntryTime,Time,Indicator)). Multiple causes event can considered Indicator (0 censored, k event cause k). right side formula specifies covariates include survival model. Cause-specific covariate effect specified cause().example, Surv(Time,Indicator) ~ X1 + cause(X2) indicates common effect X1 cause-specific effect X2. hazard optional family hazard function assumed survival model. default, "Weibull" specifies Weibull baseline risk function. possibilities "piecewise" piecewise constant risk function "splines" cubic M-splines baseline risk function. two latter families, number nodes location nodes specified well, separated -. number nodes entered first followed -, location specified "equi", "quant" "manual" respectively equidistant nodes, nodes quantiles times event distribution interior nodes entered manually argument hazardnodes. followed - finally "piecewise" "splines" indicates family baseline risk function considered. Examples include "5-equi-splines" M-splines 5 equidistant nodes, "6-quant-piecewise" piecewise constant risk 5 intervals nodes defined quantiles times events distribution "9-manual-splines" M-splines risk function 9 nodes, vector 7 interior nodes entered argument hazardnodes. presence competing events, vector hazards provided hazard=c("Weibull","5-quant-splines") 2 causes event, first one modelled Weibull baseline cause-specific risk function second one splines. hazardrange optional vector indicating range survival times (minimum maximum). default, range defined according minimum maximum observed values survival times. option used piecewise constant Splines hazard functions. hazardnodes optional vector containing interior nodes splines piecewise specified baseline hazard function hazard. TimeDepVar optional vector containing intermediate time corresponding change risk event. time-dependent covariate can take form time variable assumption effect risk time constant effect risk event time (example: initiation treatment account ). logscale optional boolean indicating whether exponential (logscale=TRUE) square (logscale=FALSE -default) transformation used ensure positivity parameters baseline risk functions. See details section startWeibull optional numeric Weibull hazard functions . Indicates shift Weibull distribution. sharedtype indicator shared random function type. 'RE' indicates association random effects included linear mixed model. 'CL' defines association predicted current level latent process. centerpoly polynomial associations , value(s) center degree 2 3 polynomial terms. methInteg character indicating type integration compute log-likelihood. 'MCO' ordinary Monte Carlo, 'MCA' antithetic Monte Carlo, 'QMC' quasi Monte Carlo. Default "QMC". nMC integer, number Monte Carlo simulations. Default 1000. data data frame containing variables named fixed, random, cor, survival subject. subset optional vector giving subset observations data use. default, lines. na.action Integer indicating NAs managed. default 1 'na.omit'. alternative 2 'na.fail'. options 'na.pass' 'na.exclude' implemented current version. B optional specification initial values parameters. Initial values entered order detailed details section. posfix Optional vector giving indices vector B parameters estimated. Default NULL, parameters estimated. maxiter optional maximum number iterations Marquardt iterative algorithm. default, maxiter=100. convB optional threshold convergence criterion based parameter stability. default, convB=0.0001. convL optional threshold convergence criterion based log-likelihood stability. default, convL=0.0001. convG optional threshold convergence criterion based derivatives. default, convG=0.0001. partialH optional vector giving indices vector B parameters can dropped Hessian matrix define convergence criteria. nsim number points used plot estimated link functions. default, nsim=100. range optional vector indicating range outcomes (minimum maximum). default, range defined according minimum maximum observed values outcome. option used Beta Splines transformations. verbose logical indicating information computation reported. what: Computation of sojourn time under specific condition — sojournTime
title: Computation of sojourn time under specific condition — sojournTime
text: function computes posterior computations joint shared random-effect model ordinal longitudinal outcomes, aka joint item response theory model. Specifically, function computes expected time spent reaching given level impairment specified one multiple items covariate profile.

title: Computation of sojourn time under specific condition — sojournTime
code: sojournTime(   x,   maxState,   condState = NULL,   newdata,   var.time,   startTime = 0,   nMC = 1000,   upperTime = 150,   subdivisions = 100L,   rel.tol = .Machine$double.eps^0.25,   draws = FALSE,   ndraws = 2000,   returndraws = FALSE,   cl = NULL,   seed = NULL )

title: Computation of sojourn time under specific condition — sojournTime
text: x object class jointLPM representing joint shared random effects model binary ordinal longitudinal outcome(s) maxState list specifying items corresponding levels defining maximum state computation sojourn time. instance maxState=list(Y = 3) compute expected sojourn time corresponding impairment Y lower equal 3. condState optional list specifying initial state start time (argument startTime) compute residual sojourn time. state can defined either single value interval. example condState=list(Y = 2) means state start time equal 2, whereas condState=list(Y = c(1, 2)) means state start time 1 2 (included). newdata one line data frame specifying covariate profile sojourn time computed. var.time character string specifying name time variable longitudinal submodel. Note time covariate included newdata. startTime numeric value specifying time residual sojourn time computed (lower bound integral time). Default 0 expected sojourn time lifetime. nMC integer giving number Monte Carlo simulations used compute integral random effects. upperTime numeric specifying upper bound integral time. Default 150 (150 years approximation infinity). subdivisions passed integrate function. rel.tol passed integrate function. draws logical indicating 95% confidence interval computed. Default FALSE. ndraws integer giving number draws used compute 95% confidence interval. Default 2000. returndraws logical indicating ndraws results returned. Default FALSE. cl either cluster created makeCluster integer specifying number cores used computation. used draws = TRUE. seed integer used draws = TRUE set random seed.

title: Computation of sojourn time under specific condition — sojournTime
text: draws = FALSE, returns single value. draws = TRUE returndraws = FALSE, returns median, 2.5% 97.5% quantiles, mean, standard deviation number removed draws (eventually due computational issues). draws = TRUE returndraws = TRUE, returns ndraws values.

title: Computation of sojourn time under specific condition — sojournTime
text: 1. Lifetime expected sojourn time: expected time reaching level k item(s) Y integral time t, 0 infinity, P(Y(t) <= k, T > t), .e., int_0^infty P(Y(t) <= k, T > t) dt. 2. Residual expected sojourn time time s: conditionally  m item(s) Y time s alive time s,  sojourn time level k item(s) Y computed :  int_s^infty P(Y(t) <= k, T > t | Y(s) <= m, T > s) dt =  int_s^infty P(Y(t) <= k, T > t, Y(s) <= m) dt / P(Y(s) <= m, T > s)

title: Computation of sojourn time under specific condition — sojournTime
text: Viviane Philipps Cecile Proust-Lima
